questions.question: When did India gain independence?
questions.answer: India gained independence on 15 August 1947.

questions.question: What was the Constituent Assembly?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly was a body formed to draft the Constitution of India.

questions.question: Who was the chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constitution?
questions.answer: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar.

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.answer: 26 January 1950.

questions.question: What was one major challenge faced by India after independence?
questions.answer: Refugee rehabilitation after the partition of India.

questions.question: What was the main reason for the reorganisation of states in India?
questions.answer: States were reorganized mainly on the basis of language.

questions.question: Which act reorganized Indian states on linguistic lines?
questions.answer: The States Reorganisation Act of 1956.

questions.question: What are Five-Year Plans?
questions.answer: Five-Year Plans are government plans designed to guide economic development.

questions.question: Which sector received priority in the First Five-Year Plan?
questions.answer: Agriculture and irrigation.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the Green Revolution?
questions.answer: To increase food grain production using modern farming techniques.

questions.question: Name one crop that increased during the Green Revolution.
questions.answer: Wheat.

questions.question: Which states benefited most from the Green Revolution?
questions.answer: Punjab and Haryana.

questions.question: What does the term 'unity in diversity' mean?
questions.answer: It means people of different cultures and languages live together as one nation.

questions.question: What is national integration?
questions.answer: National integration refers to unity among people of different regions and cultures.

questions.question: What does democracy mean?
questions.answer: Democracy means government by the people through elected representatives.

questions.question: Which leader integrated the princely states into India?
questions.answer: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

questions.question: Name one organization responsible for space research in India.
questions.answer: ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation).

questions.question: What is urbanization?
questions.answer: Urbanization is the growth of cities due to migration and industrial development.

questions.question: What was one effect of industrial development in India?
questions.answer: It created employment opportunities in cities.

questions.question: What does economic planning mean?
questions.answer: Economic planning is the systematic planning of a country's economic development.

questions.question: What is the role of Parliament in India?
questions.answer: Parliament makes laws for the country.

questions.question: What does national development refer to?
questions.answer: National development refers to overall progress in economic, social, and political fields.

questions.question: Name one factor that improved agricultural productivity after independence.
questions.answer: High-yield variety seeds.

questions.question: What is the importance of education in nation building?
questions.answer: Education develops skilled citizens and supports national progress.

questions.question: Give one example of scientific development in India.
questions.answer: Satellite launches through space research programs.
